notice-board-posting.take: You reach for the official notice. It's pinned firmly to the board with what appears to be a royal tack.
notice-board-posting.talk: You read the notice aloud. 'The kingdom is temporarily inconvenienced.' Your voice echoes across the empty square. The inconvenience is, evidently, permanent.
notice-board-posting.open: It's a piece of parchment, not an envelope. The text is right there on the front, in the kingdom's finest bureaucratic font.
notice-board-posting.push: You press the notice flat against the board. It was already flat. You've accomplished nothing, but at least the notice looks slightly more official now.
notice-board-posting.pull: You tug at the notice. The royal tack holds firm. Whoever pinned this up used a tack enchanted with the same tenacity as the curse itself.

bridge-toll-coin.take: You wedge your fingers between the cobblestones and pry at the coin. It's stuck fast, wedged in by years of foot traffic.
bridge-toll-coin.talk: You address the coin. It glints dully. Coins are famously poor conversationalists, though this one has been eavesdropping on village gossip from its crack in the ground for years.
bridge-toll-coin.open: It's a coin, not a locket. It has two sides -- heads and tails -- and neither of them opens. That's not how currency works.
bridge-toll-coin.push: You press the coin further into the crack between stones. It wedges deeper. This is the opposite of getting a coin. You are actively making yourself poorer.
bridge-toll-coin.pull: You dig at the coin with your fingernails. It shifts slightly but remains wedged. The cobblestones grip it like the village's last claim to an economy.
